scenario: Orion Insurance evaluates 5000 claims per day in Camunda 8.8 through a parallel multi-instance review task with 7 reviewers per claim. The team must end the multi-instance activity as soon as 3 reviewers approve rather than waiting for every reviewer to complete the loop.
question: Which Camunda 8 multi-instance property models this short-circuit early-termination rule?

options.text: Set completionCondition to a FEEL expression like = count(approvers) >= 3 so the multi-instance ends once 3 iterations approve
Set inputCollection to the first 3 elements of the reviewer list using FEEL so the multi-instance only iterates 3 times in total
Set loop cardinality to 3 on the multi-instance task so the engine creates only 3 reviewer iterations regardless of the original list length
Set outputCollection to a FEEL expression with a maximum size of 3 so the engine ignores iterations that would push it past the limit

a.text: Correct. The completionCondition is a FEEL boolean expression that, when true, ends the multi-instance activity early and cancels the remaining iterations.

b.text: Incorrect. Trimming the input list pre-selects reviewers; it does not implement a dynamic 3-of-7 short-circuit at runtime.

c.text: Incorrect. Loop cardinality fixes the count up front and cannot wait for 3 approvals among 7 created iterations.

d.text: Incorrect. outputCollection size is not a stop condition; the completionCondition expression is the documented short-circuit control.

explanation: The completionCondition on a multi-instance marker is a FEEL boolean expression evaluated after each iteration; when it becomes true, the engine ends the activity and cancels remaining iterations.
